name: 4. 중학 관계대명사 200
description: who/whose/whom/which/that/what 관계대명사, 관계대명사 생략, 계속적 용법 예문 200개 (중학 수준)

words.w: I know a boy who can speak English.
words.m: [who-주격] 영어를 할 줄 아는 소년을 안다. (선행사=사람, 주격)

words.w: The girl who sits next to me is smart.
I have a friend who lives in Paris.
The man who called me is my uncle.
She is the teacher who teaches us math.
I met a woman who works at the bank.
The boy who won the race is Tom.
I like people who are honest.
Do you know anyone who can help?
The doctor who treated me was kind.
He is the author who wrote this book.
The child who was lost was found.
The students who study hard will pass.
I have a neighbor who plays the piano.
The girl who is crying is my sister.
People who exercise are healthy.
The police officer who helped us was polite.
I saw a man who was riding a bike.
She is the one who gave me this gift.
words.m: [who-주격] 그녀가 내게 이 선물을 준 사람이다.

words.w: Anyone who wants to join can come.
words.m: [who-주격] 합류하고 싶은 사람은 올 수 있다.

words.w: The artist who painted this is famous.
I have a cousin who studies in Japan.
The baby who was crying stopped.
I know a chef who makes great pasta.
The singer who performed was amazing.
He is the candidate who won the election.
I need a helper who speaks Korean.
The children who came are hungry.
She is someone who always smiles.
A person who reads books is wise.
I know a boy whose father is a doctor.
She has a friend whose name is Sam.
The girl whose dog ran away is sad.
I met a man whose car is a BMW.
The writer whose book I read is famous.
He is the student whose test was perfect.
I have a friend whose mom is a chef.
The boy whose bag is red is my brother.
She is the girl whose sister is my friend.
I know a family whose house is huge.
The man whose wife is a nurse moved here.
words.m: [whose-소유격] 아내가 간호사인 남자가 여기로 이사 왔다.

words.w: The child whose toy was broken cried.
I have a teacher whose English is perfect.
The student whose paper was best won.
She is the girl whose mom works at school.
The man whose house I visited was kind.
words.m: [whose-소유격] 내가 방문한 집의 주인 남자는 친절했다.

words.w: I saw a dog whose tail was wagging.
The people whose cars were stolen reported it.
She has a brother whose job is interesting.
Anyone whose answer is wrong should redo it.
words.m: [whose-소유격] 답이 틀린 사람은 누구든 다시 해야 한다.

words.w: This is the man whom I met yesterday.
words.m: [whom-목적격] 이 분이 어제 만난 남자이다. (선행사=사람, 목적격)

words.w: She is the girl whom I love.
The teacher whom we respect retired.
I have a friend whom I trust.
The boy whom she likes is Tom.
He is the man whom I married.
The doctor whom I visited was busy.
Is she the person whom you met?
The child whom she adopted is happy.
I miss the friends whom I left behind.
The students whom he taught became successful.
She is the girl whom I dated last year.
The people whom I invited came.
He is the coach whom we all admire.
The guest whom we expected arrived.
The man whom she called is her father.
I have friends whom I can rely on.
words.m: [whom-목적격] 나는 의지할 수 있는 친구들이 있다.

words.w: The writer whom I met was humble.
She is someone whom everyone loves.
The child whom we raised is now grown.
words.m: [whom-목적격] 우리가 키운 아이는 이제 어른이 되었다.

words.w: I read a book which is interesting.
The car which he bought is red.
This is the house which I like.
I found a pen which writes well.
The movie which we watched was long.
She has a cat which is black.
The gift which she gave me is nice.
I ate the cake which mom made.
The letter which I received was long.
This is the phone which I bought.
The building which stands there is old.
I like the shirt which you're wearing.
The bike which I ride is new.
He has a watch which was expensive.
The tree which grows in our yard is tall.
I lost the umbrella which I borrowed.
The song which she sang was beautiful.
This is the dog which bit me.
The restaurant which we visited was great.
I have a book which I want to read.
The game which they played was fun.
The mountain which we climbed was high.
The box which is on the table is mine.
words.m: [which-사물] 탁자 위에 있는 상자가 내 것이다.

words.w: I saw a bird which was beautiful.
The key which opens this door is missing.
The coffee which she brewed tastes rich.
words.m: [which-사물] 그녀가 내린 커피는 진한 맛이 난다.

words.w: This is the car which I test-drove.
The shoes which I bought are comfortable.
The package which arrived is for you.
The app which I downloaded is useful.
The story which she told was sad.
The ring which he gave me is gold.
I recommend the book which I just finished.
The food which they served was spicy.
The river which flows here is long.
She has a bag which is designer.
The painting which I saw was stunning.
The jacket which she wore was warm.
The lesson which he taught was clear.
This is the computer which I use daily.
The boy that is running is Tom.
This is the book that I bought.
He is the best player that I know.
words.m: [that] 그는 내가 아는 최고의 선수이다. (최상급 뒤 that)

words.w: She is the first person that arrived.
This is the only thing that matters.
words.m: [that] 이것이 유일하게 중요한 것이다. (the only 뒤)

words.w: Everything that you said is true.
words.m: [that] 네가 말한 모든 것이 사실이다. (everything 뒤)

words.w: The dog that bit me ran away.
I know someone that can help.
words.m: [that] 나는 도울 수 있는 사람을 안다.

words.w: The cake that mom made is gone.
All that glitters is not gold.
words.m: [that] 반짝이는 모든 것이 금은 아니다. (all 뒤)

words.w: This is the cat that I found.
He is the man that helped me.
Anything that you need is here.
words.m: [that] 네가 필요한 것은 뭐든 여기 있다. (anything 뒤)

words.w: The only friend that I trust moved.
The car that he drove is fast.
The movie that won the award is great.
She is the tallest girl that I've met.
words.m: [that] 그녀는 내가 만난 가장 키 큰 소녀이다. (최상급)

words.w: This is the worst day that I've had.
He has the same car that I want.
words.m: [that] 그는 내가 원하는 것과 같은 차를 가지고 있다. (the same 뒤)

words.w: The students that passed were happy.
The song that you sang was touching.
Any book that you like is fine.
words.m: [that] 네가 좋아하는 어떤 책이든 좋다. (any 뒤)

words.w: Every child that attended had fun.
The picture that she drew is pretty.
I like the hat that you gave me.
He is the only one that understands.
The team that wins gets a prize.
Take everything that you need.
The reason that she left is unknown.
She is the best teacher that I've had.
This is what I want.
words.m: [what] 이것이 내가 원하는 것이다. (what = the thing which, 선행사 포함)

words.w: Tell me what you think.
I don't believe what he said.
words.m: [what] 나는 그가 말한 것을 믿지 않는다.

words.w: What he did was wrong.
Show me what you made.
I like what you are wearing.
What she needs is time.
Do you understand what I mean?
This is what happened.
I remember what you said.
What I love most is music.
Tell me what you saw.
That's what friends are for.
What he told me was true.
I know what to do.
This is exactly what I needed.
What matters is your effort.
Listen to what I'm saying.
Show me what's in the box.
words.m: [what] 상자 안에 뭐가 있는지 보여 줘.

words.w: What you said makes sense.
The book I read was great.
words.m: [관대 생략] 내가 읽은 책은 훌륭했다. (목적격 which/that 생략)

words.w: The man I met yesterday called me.
words.m: [관대 생략] 내가 어제 만난 남자가 내게 전화했다. (whom/that 생략)

words.w: The cake she made is delicious.
This is the house we live in.
The song he wrote is popular.
words.m: [관대 생략] 그가 쓴 노래는 인기 있다.

words.w: The dress I wore was red.
The friend you mentioned called.
The movie we saw was boring.
The car he bought is expensive.
The people I know are nice.
The food we ate was tasty.
The place I visited was beautiful.
The letter she sent arrived today.
words.m: [관대 생략] 그녀가 보낸 편지가 오늘 도착했다.

words.w: The story he told was funny.
The lesson we learned was important.
The shoes she likes are in stock.
words.m: [관대 생략] 그녀가 좋아하는 신발이 재고가 있다.

words.w: Is this the phone you wanted?
The gift I received made me happy.
words.m: [관대 생략] 내가 받은 선물이 나를 행복하게 했다.

words.w: The man she married is kind.
The project we completed was huge.
I met Tom, who is my old friend.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 톰을 만났는데, 그는 내 오랜 친구이다. (, who 부연설명)

words.w: She has a brother, who is a doctor.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그녀는 오빠가 있는데, 오빠는 의사이다.

words.w: I visited Paris, which is beautiful.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 나는 파리를 방문했는데, 파리는 아름답다. (, which)

words.w: He bought a car, which was expensive.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그는 차를 샀는데, 그 차는 비쌌다.

words.w: My mom, who is 60, still works.
This book, which I love, is a classic.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 내가 좋아하는 이 책은 고전이다.

words.w: Tom, who lives next door, is kind.
She moved to Seoul, which is big.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그녀는 서울로 이사했는데, 서울은 크다.

words.w: I called John, who was sick.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 나는 존에게 전화했는데, 그는 아팠다.

words.w: He lost his wallet, which was empty.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그는 지갑을 잃었는데, 그것은 비어 있었다.

words.w: My sister, who is an artist, paints well.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 내 여동생은 화가인데, 그림을 잘 그린다.

words.w: She likes dogs, which are loyal.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그녀는 개를 좋아하는데, 개는 충직하다.

words.w: The boss, who is strict, is fair.
I read the book, which was long.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 나는 그 책을 읽었는데, 길었다.

words.w: My uncle, who lives in LA, visited us.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 우리 삼촌은 LA에 사시는데, 우리를 방문했다.

words.w: She bought a dress, which was on sale.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그녀는 드레스를 샀는데, 할인 중이었다.

words.w: He gave me a gift, which made me happy.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 그는 내게 선물을 줬는데, 나를 행복하게 했다.

words.w: The movie, which won an Oscar, is great.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 오스카를 받은 그 영화는 훌륭하다.

words.w: Mr. Kim, who teaches English, is popular.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 영어를 가르치는 김 선생님은 인기 많다.

words.w: I ate pizza, which I love.
words.m: [계속적 용법] 나는 피자를 먹었는데, 피자를 좋아한다.
